one_time_description: Gain on disposal of tangible capital assets
economic_notes: Piapot First Nation Business Trust owns Cree Land Mini-Mart Limited Partnership and North Piapot Holdings Limited Partnership (accounted for using modified equity method). First Nation has 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings Inc. (Swift Current Casino Development). Non-controlling interest in 2544737 Ontario Limited (soft drink and ice manufacturing). Multiple specific claims initiated against Government of Canada including Flood Claim, Treaty Benefits Claim, and Misadministration Claim, with loans taken to fund negotiations and insurance to cover repayment.
notes: Province is Saskatchewan (Treaty 4 Territory). Auditor emphasis of matter regarding multiple specific claims against Government of Canada with loans secured by insurance policies. The First Nation Business Trust (GBE) is accounted for using modified equity method; its investees include Cree Land Mini-Mart LP and North Piapot Holdings LP. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($21,837,227) plus CMHC Subsidy ($768,414) plus CMHC RHI ($466,170) plus CMHC Rent ($244,080) plus CMHC RRAP ($203,355) = $23,519,246 (approximated). OSR includes rental income, FHQ Tribal Council, First Nations Trust, interest income, earnings from GBEs, and other revenues. No remuneration schedule data found in the provided text. Long-term debt includes specific claim loans (Flood, Treaty Benefits, Misadministration) secured by insurance policies with AmTrust Europe Limited. Capital lease obligations of $868,969 are separate from long-term debt. Annual debt service reflects repayment of long-term debt only ($1,588,873); capital lease repayments were $168,522 additionally.
